title: 2026 Mid-Year AI Talent Market Report
content: In 2026, the mid-year AI talent gap is approximately 1.5 million, up 20% from 2025. The ratio of senior AI talent (5+ years experience) demand to supply is 1:8. Median annual salary by role: Large Language Model Engineer 800K-1.5M CNY, Computer Vision Engineer 750K-1.3M CNY, Reinforcement Learning Engineer 850K-1.6M CNY, AI Product Manager 700K-1.2M CNY, AIGC Application Developer 500K-900K CNY. Overall salary increase rate reached 25%. Starting salaries for AI-related master's graduates now exceed 450K CNY.

title: AI Talent Geographic Distribution
content: 2026 AI talent geographic distribution: Beijing (32.5%), Shanghai (21.3%), Shenzhen (16.8%), Hangzhou (12.1%), Guangzhou (5.7%), Chengdu (4.2%), Nanjing (3.1%), Others (4.3%). Among these, Zhongguancun in Beijing and Zhangjiang in Shanghai are the two major AI talent clusters. Emerging trend: talent inflow to emerging tier-1 cities like Wuhan saw YoY growth of 45%, mainly driven by local government talent attraction policies and lower living costs. The remote work model has enabled AI talent in tier-2 cities to grow from 8% in 2024 to 15%.

title: AI Hiring Trends
content: 2026 AI hiring trends: 1) Large language model research positions declined 15%, while applied engineering positions grew 60%; 2) AI + Industry demand for cross-functional talent surged, with AI healthcare and AI finance positions growing 80%; 3) AI Safety and alignment have become a new growth area with dedicated positions emerging; 4) Remote work trend continues, with 30% of mid-to-senior positions being fully remote; 5) Stock options remain key for retaining senior talent, with Top 10 AI companies offering stock option packages averaging approximately 2M CNY.
